name: vectorstackai
documentation:
# VectorStack AI: Official Python Library
![PyPI version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/vectorstackai.svg)

The VectorStack AI Python library provides a seamless interface for interacting with VectorStack AI's powerful embedding models through their API. These embeddings are essential for a wide range of natural language processing tasks and can be easily integrated into larger AI systems, enhancing the performance of vector databases and generative AI models.

classes.name: vectorstackai.api_resources.embeddings.Embedding

methods.name: vectorstackai.api_resources.embeddings.Embedding.encode
documentation:
Creates a new embedding for the provided input and parameters.

Args:
    texts(list): A list of strings to be embedded.
    model (str): The name of the model to use for embedding.
    is_query (bool): Whether the input is a query or not.
    instruction (str): Additional instruction for the embedding process.
    **kwargs: Additional keyword arguments.

Returns:
    EmbeddingsObject: An object containing the generated embeddings and related information.

Raises:
    VectorStackError: An appropriate subclass of VectorStackError based on the error type.

A class for creating embeddings using the VectorStack API.

This class provides methods to encode text into embeddings using
specified models and parameters.

functions.name: vectorstackai.utils.raise_error_from_response
documentation:
Raise an appropriate exception based on the error response from the API.

This function dynamically maps error types to their corresponding exception classes,
extracts error information from the response, and raises the appropriate exception.

Args:
    response (requests.Response): The API response object containing error information.

Raises:
    VectorStackAIError: An appropriate subclass of VectorStackAIError based on the error type.

Note:
    This function assumes that the error response is in JSON format and follows
    a specific structure with an 'error' key containing error details.

classes.name: vectorstackai.client.Client
documentation:
VectorStack AI Client

Args:
    api_key (str): Your API key.
    max_retries (int): Maximum number of retries if API call fails.
    timeout (float): Timeout in seconds.

classes.name: vectorstackai.objects.embeddings.EmbeddingsObject
documentation:
Object returned by the Embedding API

Attributes:
    response: requests.Response
    embeddings (List[List[float]]): The list of embeddings returned by the API

classes.name: vectorstackai.error.VectorStackAIError

methods.name: vectorstackai.error.VectorStackAIError.user_message
documentation: Returns the underlying `Exception` (base class) message, which is usually
the raw message returned by OpenAI's API. This was previously available
in python2 via `error.message`. Unlike `str(error)`, it omits "Request
req_..." from the beginning of the string.

methods.name: vectorstackai.error.VectorStackAIError.construct_error_object
documentation: Constructs and returns an error object with relevant information.

Custom exception class for VectorStack-related errors.

Args:
    message (str, optional): The error message.
    http_body (str, optional): The HTTP response body.
    http_status (int, optional): The HTTP status code.
    json_body (dict, optional): The JSON response body.
    headers (dict, optional): The HTTP response headers.
    code (str, optional): The error code.

classes.name: vectorstackai.error.AuthenticationError
documentation:
Exception raised when there is an authentication error.

This error occurs when the API key is invalid, missing, or there are other
authentication-related issues when trying to access the VectorStack AI API.

classes.name: vectorstackai.error.RateLimitError
documentation:
Exception raised when there is a rate limit error.

This error occurs when the rate limit is exceeded.

classes.name: vectorstackai.error.ServiceUnavailableError
documentation: Exception raised when the service is unavailable.

classes.name: vectorstackai.error.Timeout
documentation: Exception raised when the request times out.

classes.name: vectorstackai.error.BadRequestError
documentation: Exception raised when the request is bad.
